title: fordia cauliflora
intro: Fordia cauliflora, also known as the Malayan forest oak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Fordia cauliflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
